scenario_title: Add RR at a name that already owns a CNAME
test_rationale: This test sets up a name that already owns only a CNAME, satisfies the prerequisite with name_in_use, then attempts a single A add at the same owner. That directly exercises the underspecified boundary between valid UPDATE syntax and zone-integrity enforcement under RFC 2181. The follow-up CNAME and A queries provide a binary oracle over both the reply and the resulting zone state.

group_1.behavior: Rejects the UPDATE when the add_rr would create a CNAME-plus-other-data conflict at the owner, preserving the original CNAME-only state.
group_1.post_state: alias.campus.edu. answers only with the original CNAME rrset; no A rrset is present.

group_2.behavior: Rejects the UPDATE with a different failure mode for the same semantic conflict, while still preserving the original CNAME-only state.
group_2.post_state: alias.campus.edu. answers only with the original CNAME rrset; no A rrset is present.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2181 §10.1 forbids CNAME coexisting with other data at the same owner, but RFC 2136 §3.4 and §3.4.2.2 do not specify the required UPDATE-side error code or rollback behavior when prerequisites pass and an add_rr would violate that invariant against current zone state. Implementations may therefore differ on the UPDATE reply RCODE while keeping the post-update state consistent.
